knowledge_sources.title: I sent funds to an incorrect address can you reverse it?
knowledge_sources.content:
Blockchains are decentralized networks, and transactions are strictly irreversible. As outlined in our USDCx Portal Terms of Use under the "User Error" and "No Recovery" sections, sending digital assets to an incorrect address or to the wrong blockchain results in a permanent, irretrievable loss of funds. IOG does not have custody of your funds, and, unfortunately, we do not have the technical ability to reverse transactions or recover funds sent to the wrong networks or addresses.

Unfortunately, we cannot provide manual recovery assistance in this situation because the blockchain's immutability and the bridge's autonomous design prevent us from intervening. In the future, please ensure that the selected withdrawal network on your exchange exactly matches the required network on the bridging portal before confirming the transaction.

knowledge_sources.title: Do you support Ledger or Trezor wallet?
knowledge_sources.content: At the moment, we only support trezor hard ware wallet, and we do not support the Ledger wallet

knowledge_sources.title: Two hours ago, I sent USDX from Cardano to ETH. Status: Submitting. How long should I wait? What does it depend on?
knowledge_sources.content: It needs 400 confirmations, so the process may take sometime please be patient.

knowledge_sources.content: Please open a support ticket, so we can take it from there.

knowledge_sources.title: On the webapp, Is the "Deposit Address (Base)" associated with my wallet? can it or will it ever change ?
knowledge_sources.content: Yes, the base deposit address is a unique, non-custodial address associated only with your Cardano wallet. When you deposit to this BASE address, it will send USDCx to your Cardano wallet

knowledge_sources.title: USDCx on Cardano Now Available via Circle xReserve | Circle
knowledge_sources.content:
USDCx on Cardano Now Available via Circle xReserve | Circle
Home
Already have an account?
Sign in
Sign in
Join Us
Circle Internet Financial
Feb 27, 2026
Feb 27, 2026
what you’ll learn
USDCx on Cardano is now available via Circle xReserve, enabling secure interoperability with USDC across supported blockchains.
We’re excited to share that
Cardano
is now connected to
Circle xReserve
. Through this integration, USDCx on Cardano is now available, providing a USDC-backed stablecoin to Cardano developers and users that enables access to crosschain USDC liquidity.
About Cardano
Cardano is a layer-1 (L1) Proof-of-Stake (PoS) blockchain built on peer-reviewed academic research. Launched in 2017, it uses dual-layer blockchain architecture and offers predictable transaction fees via its eUTXO model. Prioritizing security and sustainability, Cardano supports scalable smart contracts for DeFi apps and promotes global adoption for enterprises, governments, and users, especially in emerging markets.
What is USDCx?
USDCx on Cardano is a dollar-denominated stablecoin issued by a decentralized protocol on Cardano, fully backed by USDC held in Circle xReserve. This interoperability infrastructure holds funds in a non-custodial smart contract and provides deposit and minting attestations for USDCx on Cardano, enabling secure and verifiable asset issuance and crosschain transfers.
xReserve works in tandem with
Circle Gateway
and
Circle CCTP
to make USDCx interoperable with USDC across
supported blockchains
, without reliance on third-party bridges.
USDCx on Cardano
Token Name: USDCx
Token Symbol: USDCx
Mainnet address:
asset1e7eewpjw8ua3f2gpfx7y34ww9vjl63hayn80kl
Testnet address:
asset1ejelsh8crza8dyghxzsjhkjqutzr7q3dnregng
Powering new use cases
With Cardano’s connection to xReserve, developers and institutions can explore, expand, and optimize powerful new onchain USDCx use cases.
DeFi lending and borrowing with stable liquidity
USDCx brings dollar-backed stability to Cardano’s lending markets. Protocols can offer secure collateral and build multi-asset pools, while reducing the risk typically associated with more volatile crypto assets.
More efficient trading and swaps
As a dollar-denominated asset, USDCx supports deeper liquidity and more efficient
trading pairs
across Cardano-based DEXs. Users can trade, swap, and provide liquidity with greater confidence, helping fuel a more stable and capital-efficient DeFi ecosystem.
Global payments, built for compliance
USDCx enables fast, cross-border payments and settlement on Cardano. Fintechs and institutions can leverage Circle’s global network, enterprise-grade security, and compliance-first infrastructure along with fiat on/offramps to build payment and treasury solutions.
Real-world asset (RWA) settlement
USDCx provides dollar-based settlement for tokenized real-world assets (RWAs) such as real estate, credit products, and other financial instruments. This strengthens the foundation for Cardano’s growing RWA ecosystem and supports institutional-grade use cases.
Top apps and participants using USDCx on Cardano
At launch, USDCx is supported by Cardano apps like
Liqwid
,
Minswap
, and
SundaeSwap
. These DeFi apps enable features such as swapping, trading, lending, borrowing, liquidity provision, and staking on the Cardano blockchain.
Get started today
With the USDCx on Cardano integration, you can directly deposit USDC on any supported centralized exchange (CEX) into your Cardano wallet without needing to interact with Ethereum or another blockchain. Any CEX that supports USDC on Base can seamlessly transfer USDCx without requiring intermediary steps or third-party integrations.
To learn more about how USDCx on
Cardano
works, check out our xReserve
documentation
. To get started, check out
Cardano’s bridging solution
to transfer USDC from supported blockchains to USDCx on Cardano.
BACK TO TOP
Author
Team Circle
Circle is transforming the way money moves by making it more accessible, inclusive, and efficient — increasing economic opportunity and prosperity around the globe.

messages.content: What is the capital of France?
I couldn't find anything about that in my knowledge base. Would you like me to answer using my general knowledge?
Yes please

The capital of France is **Paris**. 

It's located in the north-central part of the country along the Seine River and is the largest city in France, serving as the nation's political, economic, and cultural center. Paris is home to iconic landmarks like the Eiffel Tower, the Louvre Museum, and Notre-Dame Cathedral.

Is there anything else you'd like to know?
Who won the 2022 World Cup?
I couldn't find anything about that in my knowledge base. Would you like me to answer using my general knowledge?
No thanks
No problem! Let me know if there's anything else I can help you with.
hello

Hello! 👋 Welcome to support. I'm James, and I'm here to help you.

What can I assist you with today?
hwllo

Hello! 👋 Welcome to USDCx support. I'm James, and I'm here to help you.

How can I assist you today? Whether you have questions about USDCx on Cardano, minting, burning, security, or anything else related to the bridge, feel free to ask!
hello

Hello! 👋 Welcome to USDCx support. I'm James, and I'm here to help you with any questions about USDCx on Cardano.

How can I assist you today? Whether it's about minting, burning, security, or general questions about USDCx, I'm happy to help!
I sent USDX from Cardano to ETH and status says Submitting. How long should I wait?

The process requires 400 confirmations, so it may take some time. Please be patient while the transaction completes.

If you have any other questions while you wait, I'm happy to help!
how long does a transfer take?

Based on the knowledge base, transfer times depend on the blockchain confirmations required.

For transfers from **Cardano to ETH**, the process requires **400 confirmations**, which can take some time. You'll need to be patient during this process.

The status will show as "Submitting" while confirmations are being processed.

If your transfer has been stuck for an unusually long time (like 4+ hours in a signing phase), I'd recommend opening a support ticket so we can investigate further.

Is there a specific transfer you're waiting on that I can help with?
where can i find the policy id?

You can find the official policy ID for USDCx on Cardano here:

https://cexplorer.io/asset/asset1e7eewpjw8ua3f2gpfx7y34ww9vjl63hayn80kl
how do I purchase usdcx using ada?

Hi there! 👋

To purchase USDCx using ADA, the most cost-efficient approach is:

1. **Swap ADA to USDC on a centralized exchange (CEX)** - such as Coinbase, Kraken, or Binance
2. **Convert that USDC to USDCx** using the USDCx Bridge
3. **Receive USDCx in your Cardano wallet**

This method ensures you get the best rates since you're converting through established USDC liquidity rather than going through multiple on-chain swaps.

Is there anything else I can help you with regarding the bridging process?
does your platform support stripe?

Based on the knowledge base, I can see that Stripe integration with USDCx has been **suggested as a feature request** and added to the product backlog for investigation, but it is **not currently supported**.

Specifically, a user mentioned that Stripe enabled a beta version of stablecoin payments on their account and asked about USDCx support there. The response was that it would be added to the product backlog for investigation.

If Stripe integration is important for your use case, I'd recommend opening a support ticket to express your interest — user feedback helps prioritize features on the roadmap.

Is there anything else I can help you with?
can i use coinbase to bridge tokens?

Yes, you can use Coinbase to bridge tokens via the ETH network.

However, please note that if you encounter issues during the signing phase (such as getting stuck for an extended period), you should open a support ticket so we can assist you further.

Is there anything specific about the bridging process you'd like help with?
i want to integrate with my decentralized exchange

Hi there! Thanks for reaching out about integrating USDCx with your decentralized exchange.

I'd be happy to help you get started. However, I should let you know that **we don't have public technical documentation available yet**.

Here's what I can do for you:

1. **I'll note your interest** and make sure you're notified once documentation or source code becomes available

2. **In the meantime**, you can find the official USDCx policy ID on Cardano here: https://cexplorer.io/asset/asset1e7eewpjw8ua3f2gpfx7y34ww9vjl63hayn80kl

Could you share:
- The name of your DEX?
- Your contact email or preferred way to reach you?

That way, I can follow up with you as soon as we have the technical resources ready for integration partners.

Is there anything else I can help you with in the meantime?
what is the base deposit address on the webapp?

The **Base deposit address** on the webapp is a unique, non-custodial address that is associated only with your Cardano wallet.

When you deposit USDC to this Base address, it will automatically send USDCx to your connected Cardano wallet.

This address is tied specifically to your wallet and will not change.
Do you support Ledger or Trezor wallets?

Based on the knowledge base I have available, I don't have specific information about Ledger or Trezor hardware wallet support for USDCx.

For accurate details on supported wallets, I'd recommend:
1. Opening a support ticket through the official channels
2. Checking the USDCx Portal directly for wallet compatibility information

Is there anything else about USDCx I can help you with?
Do you support Ledger or Trezor wallets?

Yes, we support **Trezor** hardware wallets.

However, we do **not** currently support Ledger wallets.
I sent funds to the wrong address, can you help?

I understand how stressful this situation must be. Unfortunately, I have to be direct with you: **blockchain transactions are irreversible**, and we cannot recover funds sent to an incorrect address.

Here's why:

- Blockchains are decentralized networks, and transactions are strictly irreversible by design
- IOG does not have custody of your funds
- We do not have the technical ability to reverse transactions or recover funds sent to wrong addresses or networks

This is outlined in our USDCx Portal Terms of Use under the "User Error" and "No Recovery" sections.

---

**For future transactions**, please always double-check that:
- The recipient address is correct
- The withdrawal network on your exchange **exactly matches** the required network on the bridging portal before confirming

I'm truly sorry I can't provide better news. Is there anything else I can help you with?
What is USDCx?

USDCx is a bridged version of USDC on Cardano. It's part of a cross-chain system that allows USDC to be used on the Cardano blockchain.

Key points about USDCx:

- **It's distinct from native USDC** - USDCx is bridged USDC, not native USDC issued directly by Circle on Cardano
- **Backed by Circle xReserve** - The USDC backing is held in xReserve, which provides the collateral for USDCx tokens
- **Cross-chain functionality** - There's bridge infrastructure connecting Cardona (testnet) to mainnet, with APIs and developer documentation currently in progress

For specific questions about minting, burning, security, or technical details, I can help with those as well based on the available documentation.
What is USDCx?

USDCx on Cardano is a dollar-denominated stablecoin issued by a decentralized protocol on Cardano, fully backed by USDC held in Circle xReserve. This interoperability infrastructure enables secure crosschain access to USDC liquidity, allowing Cardano developers and users to interact with USDC across supported blockchains.
What is USDCx?

USDCx is a version of USDC available on Cardano. It's part of a cross-chain bridging system that allows USDC to be used on the Cardano blockchain.

Key points about USDCx:
- It's backed by USDC held in Circle xReserve
- It can be minted and burned through the USDCx Bridge
- It enables USDC functionality within the Cardano ecosystem

If you have specific questions about minting, burning, security, or technical details, I'd be happy to help with those as well.
What is USDCx?

USDCx on Cardano is a dollar-denominated stablecoin issued by a decentralized protocol on Cardano, fully backed by USDC held in Circle xReserve. This interoperability infrastructure enables secure crosschain USDC liquidity between Cardano and other supported blockchains.

In essence, USDCx provides Cardano developers and users with access to a USDC-backed stablecoin that can interoperate with USDC across multiple blockchains through Circle's xReserve infrastructure.
What is USDCx?

USDCx is a version of USDC available on Cardano. It's part of a cross-chain bridge system that allows users to mint and burn USDCx tokens.

Key points about USDCx:

- **USDCx on Cardano** is backed by USDC held in Circle's xReserve
- It functions as a bridged form of USDC on the Cardano network
- The USDCx Bridge allows minting (creating USDCx) and burning (redeeming USDCx)
- It's designed to bring USDC liquidity and functionality to the Cardano ecosystem

If you have specific questions about minting, burning, security, or technical aspects of USDCx, I'm happy to help with those as well.
What is USDCx?

USDCx on Cardano is a dollar-denominated stablecoin issued by a decentralized protocol on Cardano, fully backed by USDC held in Circle xReserve. This enables secure interoperability with USDC across supported blockchains.

Key points:
- **Backing**: Fully backed 1:1 by USDC held in Circle xReserve
- **Network**: Available on the Cardano blockchain
- **Purpose**: Provides Cardano developers and users access to crosschain USDC liquidity
- **Policy ID**: You can verify the official token at https://cexplorer.io/asset/asset1e7eewpjw8ua3f2gpfx7y34ww9vjl63hayn80kl

Is there anything else you'd like to know about USDCx?

I'm James from the support team. What can I assist you with today? Whether it's questions about USDCx, the bridge, or anything else — fire away!
